what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard request record that documents and closes a design task: implement channel-attachment as a separate, file-discovered registry of rows {id, channel, attachment_type, target, added}, rather than a mutable field on the channel record, with a manifest projection and read-only channel access.
observations: The item is in state done and type request.
Title frames board_items as a parametric channel attachment via a dedicated registry.

Body specifies the registry schema and constraints: rows with id, channel, attachment_type, target, added; manifest=projection; add/remove parametric; file-disjoint from cc_channels.py; _read_channel read-only.
History shows the request was filed by ch-8djrpmsl, picked up by ch-al7jdfdr, then built and closed by ch-8djrpmsl.
Done note cites commit 385a71b, 13/13 acceptance, and verified-by-use binding of board://item-e523b30d to the live fabric channel.
Includes a follow-up note to promote attachment_type to a _CORPUS_REGISTRIES kind in suite.py.
channel and thread fields are empty.
standouts: Self-referential verification: the item records that it was bound to the live fabric channel as proof of the new attachment mechanism.
Explicit separation: attachment registry is file-disjoint from cc_channels.py and must not edit channel records.
The registry uses opaque targets and a manifest that is a projection of the rows, not a stored channel field.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter, id, address, type, state, history, and links.
Uses board:// URI addressing for the item and its authored_by session link.
Records a request lifecycle through state transitions: open -> picked-up -> building -> done.
Done note includes a commit hash and acceptance metric, acting as a closure artifact.
concerns: channel and thread fields are empty for a request explicitly about channel attachment.
The 'Fork picks up its own request' history note blurs the distinction between author_session and a separate builder identity.
A follow-up task is embedded in the done note rather than captured as a linked successor item.
notes: A closed noticeboard request that encodes the design and acceptance of a file-discovered channel-attachment registry, with the item itself serving as a live verification example.
questions: What file actually implements the registry and where do the row files live?
What is _CORPUS_REGISTRIES and how does attachment_type get promoted into it?
What do the 13 acceptance checks cover, and where are they recorded?
What is the 'live fabric channel' and how is board://item-e523b30d bound to it?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes — the file states a design/implementation request for a channel-attachment registry and records its completion, including the intended constraints and a follow-up.
apparent_themes: Separation of attachment data from channel records.
intention_signals: To establish a lightweight, file-based binding layer that lets board items attach to channels without mutating channel storage, using a row registry whose manifest is derived from the rows and whose targets remain opaque.
novelty: It treats channel attachment as an independent registry of opaque-target rows rather than a channel record mutation, and it verifies the mechanism by binding the request item itself to the channel.
contribution: A completed design decision and acceptance record for a decoupled channel-attachment registry, including the schema, constraints, commit reference, live verification example, and a pointer to a registry-kind follow-up.
summary_for_embedding: A closed noticeboard request item documenting the design and implementation of a file-discovered channel-attachment registry with rows {id, channel, attachment_type, target, added}, manifest projection, read-only channel access, and self-referential live-channel verification.
intention_for_embedding: To capture and close the request to replace mutable channel-record attachments with a separate, parametric, file-discovered binding registry.
